Synchronous inline enrollment path -- not a defect, catalog drift. Catalog expected async BEI Transfer Device Command queue entry, but employee_create uses inline dispatch. The browser CREATE response carried adms_enrollment{enrolled:true, device_sn:UDP3252900302}, which is the canonical evidence of enrollment.

Synchronous inline dispatch trivially satisfies the <60s dispatch SLA. enrolled=true was returned in the same HTTP response as the create call (same request cycle), so dispatch latency is effectively zero. Not a defect, catalog drift.

EXTERNAL_API_NOT_BROWSABLE -- verification requires Google Chat REST API access from a dedicated chat-credentialed runner. Cannot be tested via browser because Chat space messages are not rendered in the HR portal. This is not a product defect -- the Chat dispatch code exists in employee_create.py -- but the scenario catalog assertion (read Chat message body to verify content) cannot be satisfied via browser. Deferred to Wave 2 or a separate Chat-API-capable session.
EXTERNAL_API_NOT_BROWSABLE -- depends on EMP-CHAT-001 chat space message read. Same blocker: Chat REST API not accessible via browser. Deferred to Wave 2 or a Chat-API-capable runner.

BASELINE_MISSED -- the scenario required capturing missing_bio_id count BEFORE EMP-CREATE-001. A1 didn't capture it because the endpoint was unknown. A3 discovered the endpoint but the baseline is no longer recoverable (the create has already happened). The SISTER scenario EMP-COMPLETION-002, which A3 ran via API and which will be browser re-run in A5b, proves the endpoint and metric work end-to-end.
